title: Treat a Small Cut
summary: Clean, cover, and check a small cut or scrape to help it heal and lower the chance of infection.
learnerPromise: Clean and cover a small cut so it heals safely.
whyItMatters: Small cuts happen often in daily life. Knowing how to treat one helps prevent infection and speeds up healing.
realLifeUses: Helping a friend or family member with a minor cut
youWillLearn: How to rinse a cut with clean water
How to apply gentle pressure to stop bleeding
How to cover the cut with a clean bandage
How to check the cut later for signs of infection
miniChallenge: Find a bandage and practice the clean-cover-check steps on a pretend spot.
steps: Wash your hands with soap and water before touching the cut.
Rinse the cut gently under clean running water to remove dirt.
Apply gentle pressure with a clean cloth or gauze if the cut is bleeding.
Pat the area dry and cover it with a clean bandage or adhesive strip.
Check the cut daily and change the bandage if it gets wet or dirty.
completionCriteria: You washed your hands before treating the cut
You rinsed and covered the cut with a clean bandage
You know how to check the cut daily for healing
commonProblems: The bleeding does not stop after several minutes of pressure — seek help
You do not have a bandage — clean gauze or a clean cloth can work
The cut is deep or gaping — this may need stitches from a medical professional
tips: Keep a small first aid kit in an easy-to-reach place at home
Change the bandage daily or anytime it gets wet
Watch for increased redness, warmth, or swelling as signs to get medical help
